SEARCH
Package com.cellsimulation.model

Class Grid

java.lang.Object
com.cellsimulation.model.Grid
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class Grid extends Object implements Serializable
Two-dimensional playground hosting the simulated population.

A Grid owns a rectangular array of cells, each of which may hold a single Person or be empty (null). It also remembers whether the world wraps around its edges (toric mode) or is bounded.

In toric mode every position is valid: row and column indices are mapped back into range using Math.floorMod(int, int) so that negative or out-of-range coordinates wrap consistently. In bounded mode positions that fall outside the rectangle are either ignored (getCell(Position) returns null) or rejected (setCell(Position, Person) throws).

The class is Serializable so that the whole simulation state can be persisted to disk through the SaveService.

See Also:

  • Constructor Details

    • Grid

      public Grid(int width, int height, boolean toricMode)
      Creates a new empty grid with the given dimensions and edge-wrapping policy.
      Parameters:
      width - the number of columns; must be greater than or equal to 1
      height - the number of rows; must be greater than or equal to 1
      toricMode - true if the grid wraps around its edges, false for a bounded rectangle
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if width < 1 or height < 1

  • Method Details

    • getWidth

      public int getWidth()
      Returns:
      the number of columns of this grid

    • getHeight

      public int getHeight()
      Returns:
      the number of rows of this grid

    • isToricMode

      public boolean isToricMode()
      Returns:
      true if this grid wraps around its edges, false if it is bounded

    • getCell

      public Person getCell(Position position)
      Returns the person occupying the given position, or null if the cell is empty.

      In toric mode the coordinates of position are wrapped with Math.floorMod(int, int) before the lookup. In bounded mode a position lying outside the rectangle yields null rather than an exception, which keeps neighbor scans simple.

      Parameters:
      position - the position to read
      Returns:
      the person at the (possibly wrapped) position, or null if the cell is empty or out of bounds in non-toric mode

    • setCell

      public void setCell(Position position, Person person)
      Places (or removes) a person at the given position.

      Passing null as person empties the cell. In toric mode the coordinates of position are wrapped with Math.floorMod(int, int). In bounded mode an out-of-bounds position is rejected.

      Parameters:
      position - the target position
      person - the person to place, or null to empty the cell
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - in bounded mode, if position lies outside the grid

    • removeCell

      public void removeCell(Position position)
      Empties the cell at the given position.

      Equivalent to setCell(position, null) and obeys the same coordinate policy: wrap in toric mode, reject out-of-bounds positions in bounded mode.

      Parameters:
      position - the position to clear
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - in bounded mode, if position lies outside the grid

    • isInside

      public boolean isInside(Position position)
      Indicates whether the given position lies inside this grid.

      In toric mode every position is considered inside since the grid wraps around. In bounded mode the position must satisfy 0 <= row < height and 0 <= column < width.

      Parameters:
      position - the position to test
      Returns:
      true if the position is inside the grid, false otherwise

    • getEmptyNeighbors

      public List<Position> getEmptyNeighbors(Position position, NeighborhoodStrategy neighborhood)
      Returns the empty cells among the neighbors of the given position, according to the supplied strategy.

      The strategy first lists candidate neighbor positions; this method then filters them by keeping only those whose corresponding cell is empty (getCell(pos) == null).

      Parameters:
      position - the reference position
      neighborhood - the strategy used to enumerate the neighbors
      Returns:
      the list of empty neighbor positions; never null, possibly empty
